text: The river had been low all summer. I ran the same loop most mornings, past the dry weir and the bench nobody sits on, and by August the whole thing felt automatic. Then the rains came back hard, overnight, and the water rose past the old marker. I stopped to watch it. Ten minutes, easy. Didn't run the rest.

label: human
text: Made the lentil thing again. Too much cumin, honestly, but the kids ate it so I'm calling it a win. Next time less cumin, more lemon. The bread was the real hero.

text: I'm sorry. I should have called instead of texting, and I knew that while I was doing it. I'm around tonight if you want to talk. If not, that's fair too.

text: Shipped the export fix. It was the timezone thing after all, exactly like you guessed. Still chasing a flaky test on CI that only fails on Tuesdays, which makes no sense, so I'm ignoring it until it embarrasses me. Reviewing Priya's PR after lunch.

label: human
text: Nobody fished the pond anymore. The stocking program ended years back, quietly, the way those things end, and the trout that were left grew old and cunning and impossible to catch. Kids still came in July. They caught nothing and did not seem to mind.

label: human
text: The new dashboard is worse. It's prettier, sure, but it takes four clicks to do what used to take one, and the search doesn't remember what I searched. Pretty is not the job.

label: human
text: Lisbon was all hills and tile. We walked until our feet gave out, ate too many custard tarts, got lost twice on purpose. The tram was packed and slow and I loved it anyway.

text: The meeting is moved to Thursday at two. Same room. I updated the invite and attached the latest numbers. Let me know if that time doesn't work for you.

text: Assembled the bookshelf. Only two screws left over, which the internet assures me is fine and definitely not a structural warning. It's holding books. We'll see how it feels about that in a week.

text: I used to think the hard part of a project was the start. It isn't. The start is fun — everything is possible and nothing is broken yet. The hard part is the middle, when the shine is gone and you still have to show up.

text: Running ten minutes late, sorry. Grab a table if you get there first. I'll text when I'm parking.

text: Thanks for sending the draft over. I read it twice. The middle section is doing a lot of work and it shows — I'd cut the second example and let the first breathe. Happy to jump on a call if that's easier than notes.
